behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted file paths (keyboard.ts, mouse.ts, snapshot.ts, SKILL.md) and tool names (browser_type, browser_hover, browser_select_option, browser_mouse_move_xy, browser_click, browser_press_key) are named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the problem and expected behavior without prescribing specific code changes or line edits.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for presence/absence of waitForCompletion, conditional guarding by params.submit/slowly, and relevant API calls — allowing multiple valid implementations.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute Node scripts that parse handler bodies with brace counting, beyond simple text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub would fail: tests require both presence of original API calls (.hover(), selectOption, mouse.move) and absence of waitForCompletion, plus conditional guards for browser_type.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four tool changes and SKILL.md --submit documentation are tested, plus regression checks on click and press_key.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests grouped with section comment headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass) and descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies code behavior, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and tool names match across instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to look at, which is appropriate scoping; does not specify exact line numbers or code to replace.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: SKILL.md edit is a two-line localized change (comment + --submit flag on fill example).
